title: Running long working session with omp, the tab would eventually go to sleep
author: lucky13820
body:
### Description

I'm trying out https://omp.sh/ for my iOS coding. Sometimes omp will run a xcode test that takes long time to compile and run, if I don't have that tab active in Muxy, that tab will go to sleep after a while, results the test interrupted. This doesn't really happen when I was using Codex or Claude Code. 

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy
2. Open omp
3. Run a task without any immediate feedback, like running a long test
4. The tab goes to sleep when it's not selected

### Muxy Version

1.2.1

### macOS Version

26.5

title: Support for localization / 中文界面
author: BluerAngala
body: Muxy 的 UI 目前只有英文，对非英语用户不太友好。希望可以加入 i18n 支持，至少支持简体中文。可以用 Swift 的 String Catalog 来做。如果维护者觉得方向没问题，我可以提 PR 来做。

title: Crash on popover open: uncaught exception in -[NSRemoteView containingWindowWillOrderOnScreen:] (EXC_BREAKPOINT)
author: MJ10242048
body:
### Description

Muxy crashed 4 times within ~15 minutes (16:16:05, 16:16:24, 16:28:06, 16:30:04 local time), all with an identical stack. The app dies via `+[NSApplication _crashOnException:]` on the main thread — an uncaught Objective-C exception, not a memory error.

The exception is thrown while SwiftUI presents a popover: `PopoverBridge.updatePresentations` → `-[NSPopover showRelativeToRect:ofView:preferredEdge:]` → `-[NSWindow addChildWindow:ordered:]` → window-will-order-on-screen notification → `-[NSRemoteView containingWindowWillOrderOnScreen:]` throws. This looks like an out-of-process ViewBridge view (view service) in the popover's window hierarchy whose service connection has become invalid; when the popover window comes onscreen, the remote view throws and AppKit's default uncaught-exception policy terminates the app.

Exception type: `EXC_BREAKPOINT (SIGTRAP)`, faulting thread 0.

Throwing backtrace (from `asiBacktraces`, identical in all 4 reports):

```
0   CoreFoundation      __exceptionPreprocess + 176
1   libobjc.A.dylib     objc_exception_throw + 88
2   CoreFoundation      _CFBundleGetValueForInfoKey + 0
3   ViewBridge          -[NSRemoteView containingWindowWillOrderOnScreen:] + 216
4   CoreFoundation      __CFNOTIFICATIONCENTER_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_AN_OBSERVER__ + 148
5-7 CoreFoundation      _CFXNotificationPost path
8   Foundation          -[NSNotificationCenter postNotificationName:object:userInfo:] + 88
9   AppKit              -[NSWindow _doWindowWillBeVisibleAsSheet:] + 28
10-14 AppKit            -[NSWindow _reallyDoOrderWindowAboveOrBelow:] / _doOrderWindow: path
15  AppKit              _NSWindowWalkOrderingGroupInternal + 308
16  AppKit              -[NSWindow _rebuildOrderingGroup:] + 376
18  AppKit              -[NSWindow addChildWindow:ordered:] + 588
19  AppKit              -[NSPopover showRelativeToRect:ofView:preferredEdge:] + 2000
20  SwiftUI             PopoverBridge.updatePopover(popover:presentation:transaction:) closure
21  SwiftUI             PopoverBridge.updatePopover(popover:presentation:transaction:) + 2704
22  SwiftUI             PopoverBridge.updatePresentations + 1432
23  SwiftUI             PopoverBridge.preferencesDidChange + 736
24  SwiftUI             NSHostingView.preferencesDidChange() + 1704
```

Crashed-thread stack ends in AppKit layout (`+[NSApplication _crashOnException:]` ← `_NSViewLayout`), consistent with the exception being re-raised during the same runloop turn.

Notes:
- No Muxy frames in the stack, so this is AppKit/ViewBridge fragility, but it's reliably triggered from a Muxy popover and takes down the whole app; catching/guarding around the popover presentation (or avoiding a remote view inside that popover) would prevent the process kill.
- Hardware: Mac14,10 (MacBook Pro, Apple Silicon). macOS 27.0 (build 26A5378j).
- I have the full `.ips` crash reports and can attach them on request.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy 1.2.1 (build 806) and use it normally (multiple projects/tabs/panes open)
2. Open a popover in the UI (exact popover not identified — crash reports show a SwiftUI .popover presentation; happened 4 times in ~15 min, including ~19 s after a fresh relaunch)
3. App crashes as the popover window is ordered onscreen

### Muxy Version

1.2.1 (806)

### macOS Version

macOS 27.0 (26A5378j)

title: Add Mosh support for remote sessions
author: harryisfish
body:
Would you consider adding Mosh support for remote sessions? For laptop users, being able to connect to Linux servers or always-on machines like a Mac mini through Mosh would be extremely useful, since it handles sleep/wake, weak networks, Wi-Fi/VPN switching, and IP changes much better than plain SSH. This would make Muxy much more convenient for remote development workflows, especially when keeping long-running shells, agents, watchers, or TUI tools alive on a remote machine.

Mosh: https://mosh.org/

title: bug: duplicated hooks in codex
author: dergachoff
body:
## Problem

Codex can have hooks in ~/.codex/hooks.json or ~/.codex/config.toml. If hooks exist in toml, Muxy still creates a second representation in json.

Codex loads both and gives yellow warning:
loading hooks from both ~/.codex/hooks.json and ~/.codex/config.toml; prefer a single representation for this layer

May also execute commands twice

## Repro

1. Move Muxy’s SessionStart hook to to config.toml
2. Remove hooks.json
3. Launch Muxy with notifications enabled
4. Muxy recreates hooks.json
5. Restart Codex and observe the warning

title: Terminal session restore on app relaunch (especially AI agents)
author: feichuans
body:
### Problem

When Muxy is quit or restarted (app updates, crashes, system reboots), all running terminal sessions are lost — including long-running AI agent sessions like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi. Users have to manually relaunch each agent, losing conversation context and in-progress work.

This is especially painful for AI coding agents because:
- A Claude Code session may be mid-task with extensive conversation history
- Codex or Pi sessions accumulate context that cannot be trivially recreated
- Developers often run multiple agents across worktrees simultaneously

Tools like tmux/screen solve this at the Unix level, but requiring users to wrap every agent in tmux defeats the purpose of an integrated terminal app.

### Proposed Solution

Add terminal session persistence and restore on app relaunch:

1. **Automatic session snapshot** — on quit (or periodically), persist enough state per tab to resume: the running command, cwd, env vars, and ideally scrollback.
2. **Agent-aware restore** — for known AI agents (claude, codex, pi, etc.), invoke their native resume mechanisms on relaunch. For example, Claude Code supports `--resume` to continue a prior session.
3. **Graceful fallback** — for plain shell tabs, restore cwd and re-source the shell profile. For agents without resume support, show the last scrollback as read-only and offer to relaunch.
4. **Opt-in/out** — a per-project or global setting to control restore behavior (always / never / ask on launch).

This could build on the existing Layouts system — think of it as an "implicit layout" that is auto-saved on quit and auto-applied on next open.

### Additional Context

- Claude Code: supports `--resume` and `--continue` flags for session continuity
- Codex: maintains session state that can be resumed
- The existing agent detection in PR #829 already identifies which tabs run AI agents — this metadata could drive restore logic
- Related: #413 mentioned "restore running commands on restart/crash" as a desired behavior
- Related: #281 Declarative Session Manager laid groundwork but layouts are never auto-applied

title: Regression: infinite LazyVStack layout cycle hangs app on 1.3.0-beta.806 (same signature as #775)
author: deniskern
body:
## Description

The app hangs again with an infinite SwiftUI layout cycle in `LazySubviewPlacements` — same signature as #775, which was fixed in #806. It reoccurs on **1.3.0-beta.806**, which already contains that fix, so this looks like a regression — possibly reintroduced with the new sidebar layout (#828, merged 2026-06-27; my hangs started 2026-07-02).

Main thread is unresponsive for 130+ seconds at 100% CPU until macOS marks the app as Not Responding and I have to force quit. Happening daily.

## Environment

- Muxy 1.3.0-beta.806 (806)
- macOS 26.5 (25F71), Apple Silicon (Mac17,9, 48 GB)

## Evidence

- 4 `.hang` reports (3× 2026-07-02, 1× 2026-07-03) in `/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/`
- 2 `cpu_resource.diag` reports: "90 seconds cpu time over 90 seconds (100% cpu average), exceeding limit of 50% cpu"
- Latest hang: `Duration: 133.12s`, all 11 samples in the same stack
- Every session for weeks ends with `PREVIOUS SESSION ENDED UNCLEANLY` in diagnostics.log

## Heaviest main-thread stack (trimmed to the distinctive frames)

```
GraphHost.flushTransactions()
specialized GraphHost.runTransaction(_:do:id:)
AG::Subgraph::update(unsigned int)
AG::Graph::UpdateStack::update()
specialized LazySubviewPlacements.updateValue()
LazyLayoutViewCache.commitPlacedSubviews(from:to:wasCancelled:context:containingSize:)
_LazyLayoutViewCache.initialPlacement(newIndex:newPlacedSubviews:oldPlacedSubviews:wasInsertedToSubviews:context:)
_LazyLayoutViewCache.withPlacementData<A>(_:)
closure #1 in _LazyLayoutViewCache.finalPlacement(...)
protocol witness for LazyLayout.finalPlacement(...) in conformance LazyVStackLayout
protocol witness for LazyLayout.initialPlacement(...) in conformance LazyVStackLayout
specialized LazyStack<>.initialPlacement(...)
Array<A>.motionVectors(closestTo:in:avoiding:distance:)
AGGraphGetValue
AG::Graph::index_of_input_slow(AG::Node&, AG::InputEdge::Comparator)
```

Same `LazySubviewPlacements` placement churn as in #775. App binary frames are stripped in the report, so I can't name the exact view.

## Session state at hang

- 22 projects, ~52 tabs, ~46 panes, 2-3 windows
- Footprint typically 250 MB - 2.1 GB depending on session age
- Not reliably reproducible; tends to hit after the app has been running for hours with a large session (same as #775)

## Possible lead

#806 fixed conditional children inside `LazyVStack` `ForEach` (old `Sidebar` + omnibox). The new layouts from #828 add fresh `LazyVStack` usages: `TabFocusedSidebar` has a conditional child (`if !expansionStore.focusMode { TabFocusedAddProjectRow }`) plus variable-height expandable project rows, while `ProjectFocusedSidebar` kept the overlay-based fix pattern. Might be the same class of bug in the new code paths — unverified hypothesis.

Happy to share the full `.hang` reports (7-10 MB each) if useful.

After zpp entered the background for a while, it returned to the app again. The previous command record was gone. It felt like the tab page had also been deleted. Opening the app immediately created a new tab without any history

### Steps to Reproduce

1: Use muxy normally

2: Other applications later

3: Back to muxy, history is gone

### Muxy Version

1.2.1 (806)

### macOS Version

15.6 (24G84)

title: Cursor rects from inactive browser tabs leak into the active tab
author: tofunori
body:
**Bug**: when two browser tabs are open, the mouse cursor in the *active* tab takes the shapes defined by the *inactive* tab's page at the same screen position (pointer/hand, `zoom-in` magnifier, arrow), as if the hidden tab's cursor rects were still registered on the shared native view.

**Repro**:
1. Open a browser tab with a page that sets cursor styles on hover zones (e.g. a grid of thumbnails with `cursor: zoom-in`).
2. Open a second browser tab (any page — a plain text editor is enough).
3. Move the mouse around the second tab: the cursor flips between arrow, hand and magnifier at the positions where the *first* tab's hover zones are.

**Expected**: the active tab's page alone controls the cursor.

**Notes**: closing the first tab (or clicking through) makes it stop, which points at stale cursor rects from the hidden webview. Purely cosmetic — input goes to the right page. Seen on macOS (Darwin 25.5.0), current Muxy build.

title: Tab freezes permanently when scrolling with an inertia/infinite-scroll mouse in the terminal
author: NamgyungKim
body:
### Description

When I scroll inside the terminal using a mouse that has an inertia/free-spinning (infinite) scroll wheel, the scrolling stops and then the whole tab freezes. The tab becomes completely unresponsive and never recovers, so I have to expect it to keep working but instead I lose that tab entirely.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy
2. Move the mouse over the terminal area
3. Scroll using a mouse that has an inertia/infinite (free-spinning) scroll wheel
4. The scrolling stops and the tab freezes
5. The tab stays frozen and never recovers

### Muxy Version

1.2.1

### macOS Version

26.5.1

title: Files panel should respect the Default Opener (and offer "Open with…")
author: aaronv-emergent
body:
# Feature request: Files panel should respect the Default Opener (and offer "Open with…")

**Repo:** muxy-app/extensions (the built-in `files` extension) — with a possible
supporting hook in muxy-app/muxy.
**Affects:** Muxy 1.2.0-beta.795, `files` extension.

## Summary

Muxy 1.2.0 added extension **file openers** (`fileOpeners` in a manifest) plus a
**Default Opener** setting ("Open Files With" → Default Opener). Per the setting's
own help text:

> "Files opened from the terminal or the Open in IDE control go to this opener.
> Falls back to the IDE when its patterns don't match."

This works great: cmd-clicking a path in the terminal opens the file in the
selected extension opener. **But the Files panel ignores the Default Opener
entirely** — single-clicking a file in the tree always opens the built-in
`code-editor`, and the right-click menu has no opener entry. So the most natural
way to open a file (clicking it in the file browser) is the one place that can't
use a registered opener.

## Current behavior

- Click a file in the Files tree → always opens `files`' own `code-editor`
  (`muxy.tabs.open({ extension: { id: 'files', tabType: 'code-editor', data: { filePath, replaceable: false } } })`).
- Right-click a file → menu is: New File, New Folder, Rename, Duplicate, Reveal in
  Finder, Copy Path, Open Externally, Delete. No "Open in IDE" / "Open with".
- The Default Opener selection has no effect on either path.

## Expected behavior

When a registered file opener's patterns match the clicked file, the Files panel
should route the open through it — the same resolution the terminal / "Open in
IDE" control already use (`ExtensionStore.fileOpeners(for:)` /
`FileOpenerSelection`) — and fall back to the built-in editor otherwise.

## Proposal (either or both)

1. **Honor the Default Opener on tree open.** Before opening `code-editor`, the
   `files` extension resolves the file against the Default Opener; if it matches,
   open that opener's tab instead. This makes click-to-open consistent with
   terminal and "Open in IDE".

2. **Add an "Open with…" entry to the right-click menu.** A submenu listing the
   built-in editor plus every registered opener whose patterns match the file, so
   a user can pick per file without changing the global default. (If the existing
   `OpenInIDEControl` opener list could be reused per-file, that's the same data.)

A per-folder or per-extension override would be a nice-to-have, but global Default
Opener parity for the Files panel is the core ask.

## Why it matters

Extension openers are the intended way to replace built-in viewers (e.g. a
better Markdown preview). Today they're reachable from the terminal but not from
the file browser, which is where most users open files — so the feature is easy
to set up and then appears not to work.

## Repro

1. Install any extension that registers a `fileOpener` for `*.md` (e.g. a Markdown
   preview tab type).
2. Settings → Projects → Open Files With → Default Opener → select that extension.
3. Cmd-click a `.md` path in a terminal → opens in the extension. ✅
4. Click the same `.md` in the Files panel → opens the built-in `code-editor`. ❌

title: Move worktree teardown/deletion logic to backend to avoid UI freezing
author: pedrommone
body:
### Problem

When removing a worktree, muxy currently deletes the worktree directory and cleans up git state on the UI thread. On large repositories or slow filesystems this blocks the interface for several seconds, making the app feel frozen and unresponsive.

### Proposed Solution

Run all worktree teardown and deletion logic on a backend worker thread and report progress back to the UI. The UI should show a lightweight spinner or progress indicator while the operation is in progress, and only update the worktree list once the backend confirms completion or reports an error.

### Additional Context

Other destructive operations (e.g. branch deletion, repository refresh) already run asynchronously. Doing the same for worktree teardown would keep behavior consistent and prevent the UI from stalling. If the operation fails mid-way, the backend should surface a clear error message so the user knows whether the worktree was partially or fully removed.

title: Scroll failed after lost or re-gain focus
author: xanahopper
body:
### Description

when Muxy lost focus(even re-gain focus), mouse scroll in OpenCode not work. (for modern OS, mouse cursor scroll on unfocused app will act normal.
when re-gain focus, it still not work until you click terminal area one more time

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy
2. switch focus to other app or screen
3. mouse over Muxy and scroll, not working
4. switch back to Muxy, scroll still not working
5. click one more time to Muxy terminal area, scroll working

### Muxy Version

1.1

### macOS Version

26.5.1

title: Clicking a project in "All Projects" navigates into the project's workspace instead of just opening the project
author: carlovsk1
body:
### Description

When I'm in the All Projects view and click a project that belongs to a workspace, Muxy navigates into that project's workspace, switching me out of the All Projects context.

Expected: clicking the project should just open it and keep me in the All Projects view — it should not automatically switch into the project's workspace.

This is disruptive when browsing across projects from the global All Projects list, since each click pulls me out of that view into a single workspace. Ideally this would be the default behavior, or be gated behind a setting (similar to the existing `muxy.general.autoExpandWorktreesOnProjectSwitch` toggle).

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy
2. Go to the All Projects view
3. Click a project that belongs to a workspace
4. Observe that Muxy switches into the project's workspace instead of staying in the All Projects view

### Muxy Version

1.1

### macOS Version

26.6

title: Clicking a project from “All projects” switches me to my workspace view
author: pedrommone
body:
### Description

After updating to the new version, when I’m on All projects and click a project, Muxy takes me to the workspace I created instead of keeping me in the All view.

I expected to stay in the All projects view after opening a project.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Muxy
2. Go to All projects
3. Click on any project
4. Notice it switches to the workspace view

#### Expected behavior
Stay in the All projects view.

#### Actual behavior
It redirects me to the workspace I created.

### Muxy Version

Version 1.1.0 (766)

### macOS Version

26.3.1
